name: Basque Lamb Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the lamb shoulder of excess fat and cut into 1-1/2-inch chunks.
2. For a basic stew, bring all ingredients to a boil in a 4-quart pot, cover tightly, reduce heat, and simmer for 2-1/2 hours until meat is tender.
3. Alternatively, for crockpot cooking: place all ingredients into the crockpot and cook on LOW for all day.
4. When ready to serve, skim off and discard any accumulated fat.
5. This stew can be assembled quickly (less than 8 minutes) and then left to simmer for about 2-1/2 hours while you attend to other tasks.
6. Store the finished stew in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freeze it for up to 3 months.
7. To thaw, place the container in a microwave-safe dish on defrost or refrigerate for 24 hours.
8. Assemble by bringing the basic stew, vegetables, and spices to a boil in a 3-quart pot. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring twice until vegetables are almost tender.
9. Whisk together chicken stock and flour until well blended. Stir into the stew and continue simmering uncovered for an additional 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until gravy thickens slightly and vegetables are fully cooked.
